prose:
**Furan frontier-orbital analysis** — DFT, tier `standard` (**B3LYP/def2-TZVP**, density-fit) in **PySCF**, **gas phase** (no solvent model), **charge 0, multiplicity 1** (closed-shell RKS). Geometry: user-supplied `.xyz` (`/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/furan/furan.xyz`, 9 atoms: O, 4×C, 4×H); the DFT was a single point on this fixed geometry — it was not re-optimized. SCF converged in 9 cycles.

**Headline result — HOMO–LUMO gap = 6.2573602 eV** (Koopmans-style orbital gap, not an excitation energy).

Frontier orbital energies:
- HOMO (index 17): −6.3597638 eV
- LUMO (index 18): −0.1024036 eV
- HOMO−1: −7.8707441 eV; HOMO−2: −10.332024 eV
- LUMO+1: +1.4483308 eV; LUMO+2: +1.702378 eV

Total electronic energy: −230.11429 Hartree (−6261.7289 eV). Koopmans-derived descriptors: vertical IP = 6.3597638 eV, vertical EA = 0.1024036 eV, electronegativity = 3.2310837 eV, chemical hardness = 3.1286801 eV, softness = 0.3196236 eV⁻¹, electrophilicity index = 1.6684195 eV. (18 occupied of 179 orbitals.)

**Method used:** DFT (B3LYP functional, def2-TZVP basis, chemkit tier `standard`) via PySCF, gas phase, charge 0 / multiplicity 1.

**Convergence & warnings:** SCF converged (`scf_converged: true`, 9 cycles). The engine returned **no warnings array** (no warnings reported). Integrity verdict: **trustworthy = true**, status "ok" — all integrity checks passed (energy finite; SCF converged; schema header present; headline field present and numeric).

**Reproducibility:** command run was `.../cli.py frontier --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard --charge 0 --mult 1 .../furan/furan.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmp0tjgdv4k/live.json`. Note that B3LYP is an implicit-solvation-free (gas-phase) run and density fitting (RI) is on by default in chemkit, an approximation to the two-electron integrals. Orbital energies are single-point, single-geometry values (no conformer averaging).
